name: Foundation/metadata
description: GET /metadata (CapabilityStatement) conformance tests ported from Microsoft.Health.Fhir.Shared.Tests.E2E Rest/MetadataTests.cs. Covers a well-formed CapabilityStatement response advertising the patient-everything operation, HTTP 406 for an unsupported _format value, and HTTP 400 for malformed _pretty/_summary/_elements query parameters. InlineData rows that exercise the same validation-error behavior (e.g. _pretty='abc'/''/'1'/'0') are collapsed into representative cases per the task's de-duplication guidance.

test.name: metadata returns a well-formed CapabilityStatement advertising patient-everything
test.description: GET /metadata must return HTTP 200 with a CapabilityStatement resource. The server should also advertise the patient-everything operation with its canonical definition URI (warningOnly: this specific operation/URI is a Microsoft FHIR Server extension, not a base FHIR R4 requirement, so other conformant servers may omit it).

assert.description: Response body must be a CapabilityStatement
CapabilityStatement should advertise the patient-everything operation with its canonical definition URI (warningOnly: server-specific extension)

test.name: metadata with an unsupported _format value returns 406 Not Acceptable
test.description: GET /metadata?_format=blah must return HTTP 406 Not Acceptable. 406 is not one of the standard FHIR TestScript 'response' codes, so it is asserted via the numeric responseCode criteria instead.

operation.description: Request metadata with an invalid _format value

assert.description: Invalid _format must return HTTP 406 Not Acceptable

test.name: metadata with an invalid _pretty value returns 400 Bad Request
test.description: GET /metadata?_pretty=<value> must return HTTP 400 Bad Request for values that are not valid booleans. Two representative classes are covered: a clearly non-boolean token ('abc', which also stands in for the equivalent '' case), and a near-miss boolean-like token ('1', which also stands in for '0'). The near-miss case is warningOnly because leniently accepting '1'/'0' as truthy is a plausible, spec-adjacent choice for other FHIR servers.

operation.description: Request metadata with a non-boolean _pretty value ('abc'; '' collapses to the same behavior)
Request metadata with a near-miss boolean-like _pretty value ('1'; '0' collapses to the same behavior)

assert.description: Near-miss boolean-like _pretty value must return HTTP 400 (warningOnly: some servers may leniently accept '1'/'0')

test.name: metadata with an invalid _summary value returns 400 Bad Request
test.description: GET /metadata?_summary=<value> must return HTTP 400 Bad Request for an unrecognized summary token ('abc'; the empty-string case '' collapses to the same behavior).

operation.description: Request metadata with an invalid _summary value

test.name: metadata with an invalid _elements value returns 400 Bad Request
test.description: GET /metadata?_elements=<value> must return HTTP 400 Bad Request for an empty elements list (the whitespace-only ' ' case collapses to the same behavior).

operation.description: Request metadata with an empty _elements value
